content.text:
Memory stage: NoteReconciler v1.
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Decide how candidate notes should update active note memory.

        Return JSON:
        {
          "operations": [
            {
              "action": "insert | noop | supersede | retract | update",
              "candidate_index": 0,
              "target_note_id": "existing-note-id-or-null",
              "patch": null,
              "links_to_create": [
                {
                  "to_note_id": "existing-note-id",
                  "link_type": "supports | contradicts | refines | related | supersedes | derived_from",
                  "link_weight": 1.0
                }
              ],
              "reason": "short explanation"
            }
          ]
        }

        Actions:
        - insert: candidate is new reusable context.
        - noop: candidate duplicates an existing active note or is lower value.
        - supersede: candidate is a better current version of target_note_id.
        - update: patch target_note_id without inserting a new note.
        - retract: target_note_id should no longer be active because TARGET_SOURCE retracts it.

        Rules:
        - Compare semantic meaning, not wording.
        - Do not keep duplicate notes just because titles differ.
        - Prefer updating or superseding an existing note when the candidate refines the same decision, rationale, plan, or procedure.
        - Insert when the candidate captures a distinct reusable semantic fragment.
        - Use noop for summary spam, weak paraphrases, or information already captured by an existing note.
        - Use links_to_create only for strong semantic relations between notes.
        - target_note_id must be one of Existing active notes below or null.
        - candidate_index is zero-based from Candidate notes below.
        - Return one operation per candidate unless TARGET_SOURCE only retracts or updates an existing note.
        - Return valid JSON only.

        TARGET_SOURCE:
        Document title: 01. Architecture
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
Document section: 01. Architecture / 13. Note maturity .. 14. Memory repair
Document lines: 467-508

## 13. Note maturity

Чтобы `Note` не превратился в summary-store, нужен lifecycle зрелости.

Полезный паттерн:
- `fresh`
- `stabilizing`
- `mature`
- `consolidated`

Что может повышать maturity:
- note использовался в retrieval
- note подтвержден несколькими sources
- note не конфликтует с новыми данными
- note пережил какое-то время без supersede
- note важен для recurring rationale questions

Что может понижать или закрывать note:
- явный supersede
- устаревший scope
- отсутствие использования
- слабая поддержка evidence

## 14. Memory repair

Repair — это отдельный background workflow.

Что он чинит:
- duplicate notes / claims
- contradictory active items
- profile drift
- stale task states
- bad entity merges
- note spam
- budget leakage в retrieval

Почему это надо выделять отдельно:
- consolidation делает память богаче
- repair делает память здоровее

Это разные работы.

        Candidate notes:
        [0] type=DOC_DIGEST title=Note maturity lifecycle prevents summary-store drift summary=In agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, Note maturity uses a lifecycle (`fresh`, `stabilizing`, `mature`, `consolidated`) so `Note` does not become a summary-store. Maturity can rise through retrieval use, confirmation by multiple sources, no conflicts, time without supersede, and importance for recurring rationale questions; it can drop or close due to explicit supersede, stale scope, lack of...[truncated 31 chars] scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 13. Note maturity' entities=entity:<0>,entity:<1>,entity:<2> keywords=Note maturity,fresh,stabilizing,mature,consolidated,summary-store,retrieval,supersede,evidence tags=doc_digest,memory_architecture,note_lifecycle evidence=Чтобы `Note` не превратился в summary-store, нужен lifecycle зрелости.
[1] type=DOC_DIGEST title=Memory repair is a separate health workflow from consolidation summary=In agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, Repair is a separate background workflow that fixes duplicate notes/claims, contradictory active items, profile drift, stale task states, bad entity merges, note spam, and retrieval budget leakage. It is separated from consolidation because consolidation makes memory richer, while repair makes memory healthier. scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 14. Memory repair' entities=entity:<3>,entity:<4>,entity:<5>,entity:<2> keywords=Memory repair,Repair,background workflow,consolidation,duplicate notes,contradictory active items,profile drift,stale task states,bad entity merges,note spam,budget leakage tags=doc_digest,memory_architecture,repair_workflow evidence=Repair — это отдельный background workflow.

        Existing active notes:
        id=hot-path:note:<0> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Memory write model separates semantic and maintenance paths summary=In agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, the write model distinguishes two semantic write paths and two maintenance paths: Direct structured path for safely normalized facts, preferences, commitments/tasks, and status changes; Note path for important but not-yet-hard-fact material such as rationale, trade-offs, evolving plans, lessons, document digests, and contextual framing. Consolidation pro...[truncated 201 chars] scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 3. Основная модель записи .. 4. Рекомендуемая архитектура', write model subsection keywords=Direct structured path,Note path,Consolidation,Repair,semantic write paths,maintenance paths tags=memory_architecture,write_model,maintenance useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<1> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Write path operational sequence summary=In agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, the Write path persists raw input, routes it, retrieves candidate memories before extraction, canonicalizes entities, extracts claims/notes/tasks/profile updates, reconciles candidates with existing items, applies all memory operations in one transaction, and schedules maintenance such as consolidation, duplicate merge, stale cleanup, profile repair, ali...[truncated 32 chars] scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Stored memory is maintained by Background Consolidator, Memory Repair Worker, and Retention...[truncated 149 chars] scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 3. id=hot-path:note:<3> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Memory update must use retrieval and reconciliation, not append-only writes summary=In agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, memory update is defined as a four-step process: decide whether to write memory, find similar or conflicting items, convert input into typed candidates, and apply a policy such as `insert`, `update`, `supersede`, `retract`, or `noop`. Skipping retrieval/reconciliation causes duplicates, stale preferences, conflicting notes, task explosion, and summary sp...[truncated 3 chars] scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Главная мысль: update не должен быть append-only' keywords=memory update,append-only,retrieval-before-update,reconciliation,insert,update,supersede,retract,noop,summary spam tags=doc_digest,memory_architecture,update_policy useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<4> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Read path retrieval modes, budgets, and answer composition rules summary=In agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, the Read path first decides whether memory is needed for user-specific, history-aware, task-aware, rationale-heavy, temporal, or high-stakes questions, then loads core blocks, chooses Claim-first, Note-first, Mixed, or Evidence-first / evidence-fallback retrieval, respects explicit v3 budget caps, and composes answers by distinguishing verified vs provis...[truncated 142 chars] scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 12. Read path' keywords=Read path,Memory search,Claim-first,Note-first,Mixed,Evidence-first,retrieval budget,Answer composer tags=doc_digest,memory_architecture,read_path,retrieval useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<5> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Truth state and Retrieval state must stay separate summary=In agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, `Truth state` answers whether memory is current as knowledge, while `Retrieval state` answers how it should be searched and kept in the hot path. They must not be mixed: for example, a note can be `resolved` but still `warm` for rationale retrieval, and a claim can be `superseded` but remain `warm` as historical evidence. scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 8. Truth state и retrieval state' keywords=Truth state,Retrieval state,hot path,resolved,warm,superseded,historical evidence tags=memory_architecture,state_model useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<6> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Responsibilities split between User-facing runtime, Memory worker / manager, and Storage layer summary=In agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, responsibility is split so the User-facing runtime handles dialogue, tool orchestration, read-time retrieval, and cheap routing/escalation; it must not carry heavy reconciliation and repair. The Memory worker / manager owns routing, retrieval-before-update, canonicalization, extraction, reconciliation, consolidation, repair, and retention, while the Stor...[truncated 106 chars] scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 10. Разделение ответственности' keywords=User-facing runtime,Memory worker / manager,Storage layer,responsibility split,reconciliation,repair,retention,audit trail tags=doc_digest,memory_architecture,responsibilities useCount=0

responseFormat.description: Reconcile note candidates against active memory notes.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_reconciler

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.
